title: Attorney General Bonta Testifies Against EPA Proposal to Roll Back Greenhouse Gas and Vehicle Emission Standards
content: California Attorney General Rob Bonta testified before the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) in opposition to the agencyâ€™s proposed rescission of its 2009 Endangerment Finding, which determined that greenhouse gas emissions from motor vehicles contribute to air pollution driving climate change and endangering public health. If finalized, repeal of the finding by the Trump administrationâ€™s EPA would eliminate current limits on greenhouse gas pollution from vehicle tailpipes, power plants, smokestacks, and other sources, hampering future U.S. efforts to combat global warming.
author: Analysts Account
source: State of California Department of Justice
